title: python.flask.security.sql-injection.raw-query
description: Unsanitized input reaches raw SQL query through the id parameter. File: demo-vulnerable-app/app.py Line: 24
evidence: CWE supplied by source and recognized
Threat intel: SQL injection is frequently exploited and often remotely reachable.
reasoning_summary: Mapped to CWE-89 (SQL Injection). Priority score is 0.71, categorized as high. Reachability is not confirmed, so exploitation likelihood is reduced. This appears similar to existing finding SEMGREP-001, so deduplication review is recommended. WAF proposal is blocked or not eligible under safety constraints.
recommended_fix: Use parameterized queries/prepared statements, validate input, and avoid string concatenation in SQL.
safety_notes: Blocked WAF proposal: SAST-only signals cannot trigger WAF rule generation.

title: python.flask.security.reflected-xss
description: User controlled q parameter is returned as HTML and may cause reflected script execution. File: demo-vulnerable-app/app.py Line: 31
evidence: CWE supplied by source and recognized
Threat intel: XSS is common and often exploitable when reflected/stored in reachable endpoints.
reasoning_summary: Mapped to CWE-79 (Cross-Site Scripting). Priority score is 0.71, categorized as high. Reachability evidence indicates the vulnerable path is reachable. This appears similar to existing finding SEMGREP-002, so deduplication review is recommended. WAF proposal is blocked or not eligible under safety constraints.
recommended_fix: Encode output by context, sanitize untrusted HTML, and use safe templating APIs.
safety_notes: Blocked WAF proposal: SAST-only signals cannot trigger WAF rule generation.

title: python.lang.security.audit.hardcoded-secret
description: Possible hardcoded credential or API key in source code. File: demo-vulnerable-app/app.py Line: 10
evidence: CWE supplied by source and recognized
Threat intel: Hardcoded credentials are high impact when credentials are valid.

This appears similar to existing finding SEMGREP-003, so deduplication review is recommended. WAF proposal is blocked or not eligible under safety constraints.
recommended_fix: Remove secrets from source code, rotate exposed credentials, and use a secret manager.

title: python.flask.security.path-traversal
description: File path parameter is used to read arbitrary file and may allow path traversal ../ sequences. File: demo-vulnerable-app/app.py Line: 38
evidence: CWE supplied by source and recognized
Threat intel: Path traversal commonly exposes sensitive local files.
reasoning_summary: Mapped to CWE-22 (Path Traversal). Priority score is 0.77, categorized as high. Reachability evidence indicates the vulnerable path is reachable. This appears similar to existing finding SEMGREP-004, so deduplication review is recommended. WAF proposal is blocked or not eligible under safety constraints.
recommended_fix: Normalize and constrain file paths, use allowlists, and block traversal sequences.
safety_notes: Blocked WAF proposal: SAST-only signals cannot trigger WAF rule generation.

title: SQL Injection
description: SQL Injection SQL error detected from parameter id on the /user endpoint. SQL syntax error near UNION SELECT Use parameterized queries and avoid string concatenation in SQL.
evidence: CWE supplied by source and recognized
Threat intel: SQL injection is frequently exploited and often remotely reachable.
reasoning_summary: Mapped to CWE-89 (SQL Injection). Priority score is 0.81, categorized as high. Reachability evidence indicates the vulnerable path is reachable. This appears similar to existing finding ZAP-001, so deduplication review is recommended. A WAF virtual patch proposal can be reviewed by a human approver.
recommended_fix: Use parameterized queries/prepared statements, validate input, and avoid string concatenation in SQL.
proposed_waf_rule: SecRule ARGS:id "(?i:(union\s+select|select\s+.*from|or\s+1=1))" "id:100001,phase:2,deny,status:403,msg:'Proposed SQLi virtual patch for /user'"
safety_notes: WAF rule is only a proposal and requires human approval before deployment.

title: Cross Site Scripting (Reflected)
description: Cross Site Scripting (Reflected) Reflected script execution was observed in the q parameter. <script>alert(1)</script> Contextually encode output and sanitize untrusted HTML.
evidence: CWE supplied by source and recognized
Threat intel: XSS is common and often exploitable when reflected/stored in reachable endpoints.
reasoning_summary: Mapped to CWE-79 (Cross-Site Scripting). Priority score is 0.81, categorized as high. Reachability evidence indicates the vulnerable path is reachable. This appears similar to existing finding ZAP-002, so deduplication review is recommended. A WAF virtual patch proposal can be reviewed by a human approver.
recommended_fix: Encode output by context, sanitize untrusted HTML, and use safe templating APIs.
proposed_waf_rule: SecRule ARGS:q "(?i:(<script|javascript:|onerror=|onload=))" "id:100002,phase:2,deny,status:403,msg:'Proposed XSS virtual patch for /search'"
safety_notes: WAF rule is only a proposal and requires human approval before deployment.

title: Path Traversal
description: Path Traversal The file parameter can read arbitrary files using ../ traversal. ../../etc/passwd Normalize file paths and enforce an allowlisted directory.
evidence: CWE supplied by source and recognized
Threat intel: Path traversal commonly exposes sensitive local files.
reasoning_summary: Mapped to CWE-22 (Path Traversal). Priority score is 0.81, categorized as high. Reachability evidence indicates the vulnerable path is reachable. This appears similar to existing finding ZAP-003, so deduplication review is recommended. A WAF virtual patch proposal can be reviewed by a human approver.
recommended_fix: Normalize and constrain file paths, use allowlists, and block traversal sequences.
proposed_waf_rule: SecRule ARGS:file "(?:\.\./|\.\.\\)" "id:100003,phase:2,deny,status:403,msg:'Proposed path traversal virtual patch for /download'"
safety_notes: WAF rule is only a proposal and requires human approval before deployment.

title: CVE-2017-18342

evidence: CWE supplied by source and recognized
Threat intel: No explicit threat-intelligence signal found in local MVP dataset.
reachability_reason: SCA package finding requires dependency reachability confirmation
